title: Pass 5.G.1: ferrotorch-optim per-step Vec<f64> heap alloc (gigabytes for non-foreach optimizers)
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-optim audit, PERF_OBVIOUS.

All optimizers without foreach (Muon, KFAC, LBFGS, SparseAdam, Adafactor, RProp, …): every step heap-allocates two Vec<f64> workspaces (param_data, grad_data) of full numel size and discards them. For a 7B-param model this is gigabytes of temporary CPU allocation per step.

Bundle adjacent perf findings:
- lbfgs.rs:399-401 — Vec::remove(0) on three parallel s/y/rho histories is O(history_size) per step; trivial fix VecDeque
- lbfgs.rs:331,356,583 + natural_gradient.rs:247,261 — full f64 dot products and triple-nested loops, no slice iterators / SIMD hints; KFAC update_factors hot
- adam.rs:686-693 — gpu_to_cpu Vec<u8> reinterpretation copies twice (bytes→f32→f64)

Acceptance:
- Pre-allocate persistent workspaces per optimizer state; reuse across steps
- VecDeque for LBFGS history
- Discriminating bench: allocator counter shows ≤1 alloc per step on default optimizer.
